{"key_event":"Ley Maldita amid expanding mass politics","reasoning":"State power stayed very high: the Ley Maldita, surveillance, emergency powers, and technocratic economic management increased coercive and administrative reach, but inflation, urban housing failures, and repeated fare-price riots exposed real limits. Civil society was dented by Communist proscription and repression, yet women’s suffrage, the CUT, new party coalitions, labor unrest, and organized poblador action kept society dense and increasingly mobilized.","society_change":0.1,"society_power":7.8,"state_change":0.1,"state_power":8.8}
